name: Erythromycin
executiveSummary: Macrolide. Infections caused by susceptible bacteria; neonatal ophthalmic prophylaxis; gastric motility stimulation in selected cases.
indications:
## Indications

- Infections caused by susceptible bacteria (respiratory, skin, otitis).
- Prophylaxis of neonatal ophthalmia (ophthalmic ointment).
- Gastric motility stimulation per protocol.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.dose: 250–500 mg PO every 6 h or 500 mg–1 g IV every 6 h.

pediatrico.notes: Adjust in hepatopathy. Interactions with cisapride, terfenadine and other CYP3A4 substrates.

neonatal.dose: Ocular prophylaxis: 0,5% ointment per protocol. IV/PO per NICU indication.

stability:
## Stability

- IV unstable; use promptly after reconstitution.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, QT prolongation, hepatotoxicity, hypersensitivity reactions.

bibliography.citation: Infectious Diseases Service, Infection Prevention and Control. UCIP 2026 — Dilution and stability guide.
